title: Teriyaki Ginger-Glazed Salmon with Sesame Bok Choy
description: Home Chef customer favorite — flaky salmon fillets glazed in a homemade teriyaki-ginger sauce, served alongside sesame-tossed bok choy and steamed rice. A restaurant-quality dinner in under 30 minutes.
instructions: Cook rice according to package directions.
Make teriyaki sauce: whisk together soy sauce, mirin, honey, minced ginger, minced garlic, and cornstarch in a small bowl. Set aside.
Pat salmon fillets dry and season lightly with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Add salmon skin-side up; cook 3–4 minutes until golden. Flip and cook 2–3 more minutes until cooked through.
Pour teriyaki sauce over salmon. Cook 1–2 minutes, spooning sauce over the fish, until the sauce thickens and glazes the salmon. Remove to a plate.
In the same pan, heat remaining oil. Halve bok choy lengthwise and add cut-side down. Cook 2 minutes until lightly charred. Flip, add a splash of water, cover, and steam 1–2 minutes until tender.
Drizzle bok choy with sesame oil and toss with sesame seeds.
Serve salmon over rice with bok choy alongside. Garnish with sliced scallions and extra sesame seeds.
tips: Don't move the salmon once it's in the pan — let it develop a golden crust before flipping.
Mirin adds authentic sweetness; if you don't have it, substitute with 2 tbsp dry sherry or white wine plus 1 tsp extra honey.
The cornstarch in the sauce is key to getting that glossy, restaurant-style glaze.
This glaze also works beautifully on chicken thighs or shrimp.
